Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;   CREATE TABLE Book(    isbn CHAR(13),    title TEXT NOT NULL,    author TEXT NOT NULL,    PRIMARY KEY(isbn),    UNIQUE(isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy(    isbn CHAR(13),    serial_number INT,    weight INT,    bookcase INT,    PRIMARY KEY(serial_number, isbn),    FOREIGN KEY (isbn)       REFERENCES Book(isbn)       ON DELETE CASCADE       ON UPDATE CASCADE       NOT DEFERRABLE );

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book VALUES ('1234567890123', 'coolheid', 'noudnation'); INSERT INTO Book VALUES ('1234567890123', 'fiets', 'widad');

Q Text: For your database, give an SQL statement that shows that constraint 4 is handled correctly.
Answer: DELETE FROM Book WHERE isbn = '9781292025823';

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn INT, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (isbn), UNIQUE (isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn INT,  serial_number INT, weight INT, bookcase INT, UNIQUE (isbn, serial_number),  FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON delete cascade ON update cascade );   --INSERT INTO Book --VALUES --(978129202582, 'A first course in Database Systems', 'Jennifer Widom');   --INSERT INTO Copy --VALUES --(978129202582, 123, 1, 3);   --UPDATE Book SET isbn = 123 WHERE isbn = 978129202582; --DELETE FROM Book WHERE isbn = 123;

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (  isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT );   CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn TEXT, serial_number INT PRIMARY KEY, weight INT, bookcase INT, FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book(isbn VARCHAR NOT NULL,                                      title TEXT NOT NULL,                                      author TEXT NOT NULL,                                      PRIMARY KEY(isbn),                                      UNIQUE(isbn));     CREATE TABLE Copy(isbn VARCHAR NOT NULL,                                     serialnumber INT NOT NULL,                                      weight INT NOT NULL,                                      bookcase INT,                                      CONSTRAINT FK_Book FOREIGN KEY (isbn)                                     REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON update cascade ON delete cascade,                                     PRIMARY KEY(serialnumber),                                     UNIQUE(serialnumber));

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION max_billion    CHECK ( NOT EXISTS (    SELECT SUM(C.weight) as total_weight   FROM Copy C   HAVING total_weight > 1000000000 )  )

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION max_billion    CHECK ( NOT EXISTS (    SELECT SUM(C.weight) as total_weight, C.bookcase   FROM Copy C   GROUP BY C.bookcase   HAVING total_weight > 1000000 )  )

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn TEXT NOT NULL, 	title TEXT, 	author TEXT,     PRIMARY KEY(isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy ( 	isbn TEXT NOT NULL, 	serial_number TEXT, 	weight NUMERIC (5,2), 	bookcase INTEGER,     UNIQUE (isbn, serial_number)     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn)         ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (    isbn NUMERIC(13,0),    title TEXT NOT NULL,    author TEXT,    PRIMARY KEY(isbn),    UNIQUE (isbn) )   CREATE TABLE Copy (    isbn NUMERIC(13,0),    serial_number INT,    weight INT,    bookcase INT,    PRIMARY KEY(serial_number),    UNIQUE(isbn, serial_number),    FOREIGN KEY(isbn)       REFERENCES Book(isbn)       ON DELETE CASCADE   -- if book is deleted delete, associated copies       ON UPDATE CASCADE   -- if book's isbn is updated, update isbn of associated copies   CREATE ASSERTION book_of_copy_exists    CHECK (        NOT EXISTS (          SELECT *          FROM Copy C          WHERE NOT EXISTS (             SELECT *             FROM Book B             WHERE B.isbn = C.isbn       )                )       )

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book(     isbn INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,     title TEXT,     author TEXT );     CREATE TABLE Copy(   isbn INTEGER,    serial_number INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,   weight INTEGER   bookcase INTEGER,    FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn));

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: pragma foreign_keys = on; create table Book( isbn varchar, title varchar, author varchar, primary key(isbn));   create table Copy( isbn varchar, bookNr int, weight int, bookcase int, foreign key(isbn) references Book(isbn) on update cascade  on delete cascade, unique( isbn, bookNr));

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: Create table Book( isbn CHAR(20) PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT NOT NULL)   Create table Copy( isbn CHAR(20) NOT NULL, serial_number INT PRIMARY KEY, weight INT NOT NULL CHECK (weight > 0), bookcase INT NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE) ;

A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book(       isbn TEXT,       title TEXT NOT NULL,       author TEXT,       PRIMARY KEY (isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy(       isbn TEXT,       serial_number INT NOT NULL,       weight INT NOT NULL,       bookcase INT,       UNIQUE (isbn, serial_number),       FOREIGN KEY (isbn)            REFERENCES Book(isbn)            ON UPDATE CASCADE            ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book VALUES('1', 'aa', 'bb'); INSERT INTO Book VALUES('1', 'cc', 'dd');

Q Text: Let's assume that the book table contains the following row (and no other rows):     ('978129202582', 'A first course in Database Systems', 'Jennifer Widom') Give an SQL statement that violates constraint 2.
Answer: INSERT INTO Copy VALUES('1', 5, 200, 7);

Q Text: Let's assume that we have two copies of Jennifer Widom's book, i.e., the table Copy has the following two rows:    [ ('9781292025827', 1, 200, 81),      ('9781292025827', 2, 200, 81) ] Give an SQL update statement that shows that condition 3 is handled correctly.
Answer: UPDATE Book SET isbn = '3' WHERE isbn = '9781292025827';

Q Text: For your database, give an SQL statement that shows that constraint 4 is handled correctly.
Answer: DELETE FROM Book WHERE isbn = '3';

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy (or recreate Copy with the added constraint) that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Copy(       isbn TEXT,       serial_number INT NOT NULL,       weight INT NOT NULL,       bookcase INT,       UNIQUE (isbn, serial_number),       FOREIGN KEY (isbn)            REFERENCES Book(isbn)            ON UPDATE CASCADE            ON DELETE CASCADE,       CONSTRAINT check_weight CHECK (weight >= 10 AND weight <= 1000) );

Q Text: Write an assertion that makes sure that somewhere in our library there exists a book copy written by 'Jennifer Widom' NB PostgreSQL and SQLite do not support assertions yet. PostgreSQL does check the syntax of your query.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION book_copy_written_by_Jennifer_Wisdom CHECK (EXISTS (        SELECT *        FROM Book, Copy        WHERE Author = 'Jennifer Widom'        AND Book.isbn = Copy.isbn) )

Q Text: Create an assertion that states that the total amount of book copies in our library should not exceed 1 billion grams.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION not_exceed_one_billion_grams CHECK (NOT EXISTS (          SELECT SUM(weight) AS total          FROM Copy          WHERE total > 1,000,000,000) )

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.
Answer: CREATE ASSERTION no_bookcase_with_more_than_one_million_grams CHECK (NOT EXISTS (        SELECT SUM(weight) AS total        FROM Copy        WHERE total > 1,000,000        GROUP BY bookcase) )

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE Table Books(   isbn INT PRIMARY KEY,   title TEXT,   author TEXT   ); CREATE Table Copy(   isbn INT,   serial_number int,   weight int,   bookcase INT,   UNIQUE(isbn,serial_number),   FOREIGN KEY(isbn)   REFERENCES Books(isbn)   ON UPDATE CASCADE On DELETE CASCADE,   CONSTRAINT isbn CHECK ( EXISTS (Select * From Books Where Books.isbn = Copy.isbn))  );
